name: burstcode
description: Windsurf-style autonomous coding agent powered by local OpenAI-compatible LLMs.

commands.title: BurstCode: Show Background Explorer Activity Log

burstcode.llm.chat.baseURL.description: Chat profile · OpenAI-compatible base URL (e.g. http://localhost:11434/v1).

burstcode.llm.chat.apiKey.description: Chat profile · API key (optional for most local servers).

burstcode.llm.chat.model.description: Chat profile · active model id used for chat requests.

burstcode.llm.chat.contextWindow.description: Chat profile · total context window in tokens.

burstcode.llm.chat.allowSelfSignedCerts.description: Chat profile · skip TLS certificate verification for the baseURL.

burstcode.llm.chat.models.description: Chat profile · manually-curated model ids shown in the picker alongside ids fetched from /v1/models.

burstcode.llm.background.inherit.markdownDescription: **Background profile** · When enabled, the Background Explorer uses the same endpoint/model as the chat profile. Leave disabled and set `#burstcode.llm.background.baseURL#` to use a separate endpoint.

burstcode.llm.background.baseURL.description: Background profile · OpenAI-compatible base URL. Leave empty to inherit from the chat profile when `inherit` is false.

burstcode.llm.background.apiKey.description: Background profile · API key. Leave empty to inherit from the chat profile when `inherit` is false.

burstcode.llm.background.model.description: Background profile · model id used for background analysis. Leave empty to fall back to the first entry of `models`, then the chat model.

burstcode.llm.background.contextWindow.description: Background profile · total context window in tokens.

burstcode.llm.background.allowSelfSigned.description: Background profile · skip TLS certificate verification for the baseURL.

burstcode.llm.background.models.description: Background profile · manually-curated model ids for the picker.

burstcode.git.autoCheckpoint.description: Before applying any BurstCode edit, snapshot the working tree to refs/burstcode/checkpoints/<timestamp> so the change can be rolled back later.

burstcode.agent.maxIterations.description: Maximum tool-calling iterations per user request.

burstcode.agent.autoContinueOnLength.description: When the LLM stops mid-turn because of the output token limit (finish_reason=length), automatically prompt it to continue instead of ending the turn.

burstcode.agent.maxAutoContinues.description: Maximum number of consecutive auto-continues before giving up. Resets whenever the model successfully calls a tool.

burstcode.agent.autoResumeOnStreamError.description: When the LLM stream is interrupted by a transient error (network drop, server reset, HTTP 5xx, etc.), automatically retry the in-flight turn instead of ending the run with an error.

burstcode.agent.maxAutoResumes.description: Maximum number of consecutive auto-resumes after a stream interruption before surfacing the error. The counter resets after a successful streaming turn.

burstcode.agent.maxStuckRepeats.description: Number of CONSECUTIVE identical tool-call turns that must be observed before escalating to an interactive prompt. The 1st repeat (2 identical turns) always injects an automatic self-correction nudge; once the counter reaches this value (default: 3 identical turns) the agent asks you to continue, give a hint, or stop.

burstcode.agent.autoContinueOnPrematureS.description: When the model stops after tool use without a clear final answer, automatically continue the run instead of silently ending.

burstcode.agent.maxPrematureStopContinue.description: Maximum number of automatic continuations after suspicious early stops in one user request.

burstcode.agent.subagentMaxIterations.description: Maximum tool-calling iterations per launched sub-agent.

burstcode.agent.maxConcurrentSubagents.description: Maximum number of sub-agents that may run concurrently inside one foreground agent request.

burstcode.agent.maxSubagentTasksPerCall.description: Maximum number of tasks accepted by one launch_subagent tool call.

burstcode.agent.enableWriteSubagents.description: Allow launch_subagent to run independent write tasks that can call propose_edit within explicit allowedFiles scopes.

burstcode.diag.webviewProbe.description: Diagnostic only — when true, the BurstCode chat panel renders a red banner at the bottom listing the hit-test result of every interactive button (probes for invisible overlays / unclickable controls) and logs every click. Reload the window after toggling. Leave off in normal use.

burstcode.lsp.maxWaitMs.description: Maximum time to wait for a language server to be ready.

burstcode.context.outlineBaseDepth.description: Recursion depth used when generating the workspace outline injected into the agent's system prompt. Higher = more files visible but more tokens spent.

burstcode.context.outlineSrcDepth.description: Recursion depth applied to source-like directories (src, lib, app, packages, ...) so the model can see deeper into them.

burstcode.context.outlineMaxBytes.description: Soft cap (in characters) on the workspace outline embedded in the system prompt. Outlines exceeding this are truncated and the agent is told to drill in via workspace_outline.

burstcode.context.outlineExtraExcludes.description: Additional directory basenames to exclude from the workspace outline (in addition to defaults like node_modules, dist, out, .git).

burstcode.background.enabled.description: Enable the background Code Explorer. When the IDE is idle (no chat run in progress and no recent edits), BurstCode will pick one source file at a time, read it, and write a per-file summary, suspected-bug log, and auto-generated unit tests for uncertain points to the workspace's output directory. Pauses automatically while the user is typing or while a chat request is being processed.

burstcode.background.idleThresholdMs.description: Idle gap (ms) after the latest real text edit before a background analysis cycle starts. Cursor/editor/window changes only pause an already-running background job; they do not keep postponing the next cycle.

burstcode.background.minIntervalMs.description: Minimum gap (ms) between successive background cycles, even when the user has been idle the whole time.

burstcode.background.batchCharBudget.description: Soft character cap per full-scan batch. The background explorer enumerates all candidate source files, sorts by directory, and greedy-packs them into batches whose combined contents stay under this budget (one LLM call per batch). Roughly 4 chars ≈ 1 token; tune to your model's context window. Lower this for small-context models; raise it for big-context models to make each cycle do more work in fewer calls.

burstcode.background.batchMaxFiles.description: Hard cap on files per full-scan batch. Prevents pathological cases where many tiny files would otherwise pile into a single batch and slow down per-batch latency.

burstcode.background.maxConcurrentTopics.description: How many investigation topics to run in parallel within a single cycle. Each topic uses its own AgentLoop and therefore its own LLM streams. Increase to use the full cycle window when your endpoint allows multiple concurrent requests; lower to 1 for strictly sequential execution.

burstcode.background.includeExtensions.description: File extensions (no leading dot) the background explorer is allowed to read and analyse.

burstcode.background.maxFileBytes.description: Skip files larger than this many bytes. Keeps each LLM call from blowing past the model's context window.

burstcode.background.outputDir.description: Workspace-relative directory where the explorer writes docs/, bugs.md, tests/, and state.json. Created on first run; safe to delete.

burstcode.background.perFileTimeoutMs.description: Base timeout (ms) for a single background LLM call (default 5 min). Full-scan batches and topic investigations both run an entire request and use 4× this value (default 20 min) so a slow batch / multi-file agent loop doesn't get killed prematurely. Raise this if you're driving the explorer with a small / slow / CPU-only model.

burstcode.background.runGeneratedTests.description: When enabled, the background explorer will spawn the appropriate test runner (vitest/jest for TS/JS via npx, pytest for Python) on each generated unit test file and record pass/fail/skip results to verifications.md and a per-test .result.md. Off by default — running auto-generated code touches your shell and dependencies; review the test files first before enabling on untrusted projects.

burstcode.background.testRunTimeoutMs.description: Per-test execution timeout (ms). The runner is killed and recorded as 'error: timed out' if it exceeds this duration.

burstcode.shell.enabled.description: Allow the agent to execute shell commands via the run_shell tool. Disable to remove the tool from the agent's toolbox entirely (existing chats won't be able to spawn processes).

burstcode.shell.autoApprove.description: Skip the per-command approval prompt and execute every run_shell call immediately. Convenient for trusted local workflows; LEAVE OFF unless you understand the risk — the agent runs with the same privileges as VS Code.

burstcode.shell.defaultTimeoutMs.description: Default timeout (ms) for a single run_shell invocation when the model does not specify one. Hard cap is 600000 (10 minutes). Processes that exceed the timeout are killed and the call returns isError=true.

burstcode.shell.maxOutputBytes.description: Maximum bytes of stdout / stderr (each, separately) captured per run_shell call before the tail is dropped. Output is further compressed before being stored in context (see burstcode.agent). Raise this if you routinely redirect large build logs; lower it on memory-constrained machines. Hard cap is 524288 (512 KB).
